purpose: Machine-readable governance ledger for V2 live-job tranche accounting. This file does not authorize live jobs by itself.

approvalPointer.source: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
approvalPointer.summary: Captain reset/extended the V2 HighJump live-job budget to 18 after HJ38 produced a complete internal alpha report with a concrete source-usefulness defect.

latestReset.reason: Captain provided a fresh 18-job budget after HJ38 so the team can continue using stronger HighJump validation toward complete and useful V2 reports without artificial budget friction.

currentTrancheAccounting.note: Captain reset the HighJump continuation budget to 18 after HJ38. HJ39-HJ43 consumed five jobs. HJ43 restored complete internal alpha report generation with 8 Source Material records and 2 EvidenceItems, but the report still lacks direct current-stock/population evidence and cites only historical inflow plus total population context. The next step should raise the source-acquisition quality bar toward direct current-stock material, likely via a bounded official document/spreadsheet/PDF or source-material strategy rather than another recall/cap tweak.

unreconciledHistoricalNote.summary: The supplied 2026-05-21 HighJump handover claimed a separate 20-slot budget that was not reflected in this ledger. The 2026-05-22 Captain reset to 18 supersedes prior remaining balance for future accounting only; it does not validate or reclassify any unledgered historical job.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ34 reran the German asylum aggregate input after the balanced quantitative query-discovery repair. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held, but the hidden chain repeated the HJ33 regression: Source Material records 0, W4-G w3b_not_completed, W4-H w4g_not_positive, W5 blocked_pre_execution, source-content packets 0. This disproves the query-planning hypothesis for this claim family; HJ35 returns query planning to HJ32 behavior and repairs W5 extraction selectivity instead.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ35 reran the German asylum aggregate input after reverting the query-planning overcorrection and repairing W5 quantitative extraction. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held, but Source Material records remained 0, W4-G w3b_not_completed, W4-H w4g_not_positive, W5 blocked_pre_execution, source-content packets 0. This points to the W3-B source-material strong-anchor gate or provider materialization, not W5 extraction.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ36 reran the German asylum aggregate input after bounded Serper search-preview Source Material admission. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held. Source Material records rose to 2, W4-G sidecars 2, W4-H packets 1, W4-I structurally eligible/execution denied, and W5 executed with 2 source-content packets / 390 input-packet bytes but returned no_extractable_evidence. Hidden artifacts showed two Serper preview records only, so HJ37 repairs source depth by bounded linked-page materialization rather than loosening W5.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ37 reran the German asylum aggregate input after bounded Serper linked-page Source Material. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held. Durable admin summary showed Source Material records 2, then W4-G blocked before sidecars with w4c_not_completed; W4-H/W4-I/W5 were consequently blocked and source-content packets were 0. The observed failure is a downstream corpus gate still accepting only accepted_json, so 13bd3c39 amends the existing W4C/W4D content-type guards to admit accepted_text without changing extraction semantics.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ37B reran the German asylum aggregate input after accepted_text downstream admission. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held. Durable admin summary showed Source Material records 1, then W4-G blocked with source_material_text_oversized because the bounded linked-page Source Material carried truncationApplied=true at the 4096-byte cap. The observed failure is a downstream cap/provenance mismatch, so f479df9f preserves the truncation flag through W4-A/W4-C/W4-D/W4-G/W4-H while keeping the same byte caps and hash checks.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"; superseded by the later fresh reset to 18 after this HJ37C result.
consumption.note: HJ37C reran the German asylum aggregate input after bounded truncation preservation. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held. Durable admin summary showed Source Material records 1, W4-G bounded_corpus_text_sidecar_created_extraction_gate_closed with 1 sidecar, W4-H bounded_extraction_input_packet_created_extraction_execution_closed with 1 packet, W4-I structurally eligible, and W5 hidden_no_extractable_evidence with 1 source-content packet and 4096 input bytes. The observed stop is no longer a downstream gate mismatch; it is source depth/usefulness or W5 selectivity, so fb8b50f3 first amends the existing Serper aggregate cap before changing W5.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ38 reran the German asylum aggregate input after widening the bounded Serper linked-page aggregate cap. The run stayed on claimboundary-v2 and public/default containment held: public/default reportMarkdown stayed null, schema stayed 4.0.0-cb-precutover, cutover stayed blocked_precutover, and issue stayed report_damaged. Authenticated admin reportMarkdown was 3561 characters and produced a complete internal alpha review with UNVERIFIED / truth 0 / confidence 0. The observed report defect is source usefulness: the report relied on annual asylum-application flow evidence rather than direct current stock evidence for the claimed 235000+ asylum-domain population.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ39 reran the German asylum aggregate input after bounded Serper per-query fan-in. Public/default containment held. The admin stop summary regressed before Source Material: records 0, W4-G w3b_not_completed, W4-H w4g_not_positive, W5 blocked_pre_execution, source-content packets 0. This disproves per-query fan-in as sufficient and points back to generic query measurement-frame intent/source-material usefulness.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ40 reran the German asylum aggregate input after the generic measurement-frame query-intent repair. Public/default containment held: public/default result remained 4.0.0-cb-precutover / blocked_precutover / report_damaged with reportMarkdown null. Authenticated admin reportMarkdown was 3591 characters and produced a complete internal alpha report, but quality remained poor: it relied on one OpenAlex gender-composition item and did not surface direct current-stock evidence for the claimed 235000+ asylum-domain population. HJ41 therefore amends only the structural Source Material merge order so OpenAlex abstracts cannot prefix-dominate Serper/Wikimedia material.

consumption.approvalPointer: Captain message in current Codex thread: "New job budget: 18"
consumption.note: HJ41 reran the German asylum aggregate input after structural Source Material provider-order balancing. Public/default containment held: claimboundary-v2 stayed 4.0.0-cb-precutover / blocked_precutover / report_damaged with default reportMarkdown null. Authenticated admin reportMarkdown was 3838 characters and produced a complete internal alpha report, but it cited June 2025 monthly inflow evidence (2213 new applications) rather than direct current-stock/population evidence for the claimed 235000+ asylum-domain population. The next step should improve acquisition/source selection toward direct stock material before spending another validation job.

consumption.note: HJ42 reran the German asylum aggregate input after stronger bounded Serper recall. Public/default containment held. The run produced only a 1170-character admin stop summary: Candidate Provider Network status candidate_provider_network_damaged_structural, damagedReason candidate_runtime_query_coverage_invalid, query outcomes 0/0 candidates and one timeout, Source Material records 0, W4-G w3b_not_completed, W5 blocked_pre_execution. This shows W3-B still gates provided Serper Source Material behind Wikimedia candidate-provider-network completion.

consumption.note: HJ43 reran the German asylum aggregate input after the Serper carry repair. Public/default containment held. Authenticated admin reportMarkdown was 4748 characters and produced a complete internal alpha report. Hidden artifacts showed Source Material completed with 8 records, W5 extracted 2 EvidenceItems, and the report cited historical cumulative asylum-domain inflow plus total Swiss population context. The report still lacks direct current-stock/population evidence for the 235000+ asylum-domain population claim.

exceptionAccounting.reason: Two HJ11 jobs consumed budget without exercising V2 because they used the V1 claimboundary variant. Steer-Co then authorized one corrective HJ11 V2 canary, one HJ12 prompt-contract validation canary, one HJ13 OpenAlex balance canary, one HJ14 Wikimedia depth canary, one HJ15 OpenAlex fan-in canary, one HJ16 aggregate-cap recalibration canary, and one HJ17 W5 output-budget validation canary to avoid an artificial report-creation stop; all are recorded transparently.
exceptionAccounting.nextLiveJobRequirement: Any further HighJump live job requires a committed repair, runtime refresh, clean git status, route/runtime preflight, API/Web runtime commit match, and explicit Steer-Co/Captain budget reconciliation.
